title: corydalis caseana subsp. brachycarpa
intro: Corydalis caseana subsp. brachycarpa is a plant species within the family Papaveraceae, commonly known as the Himalayan corydalis or corydalis. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as pain relief, reducing inflammation, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Corydalis caseana subsp. brachycarpa is often prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, or capsules, and its root is used in traditional medicine to create remedies for various health conditions.
